campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to support youth scholarships in the recreation department. The council also discussed extending an interim ordinance and moving forward with plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen for youth recreation scholarships
Extension of interim ordinance #2326 (specific rules being renewed)
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge project over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant applications being submitted to fund the Hamilton Avenue bridge

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council approved funding decisions for local community events and grants. They decided which organizations would receive money for events, how much each would get, and made some updates to the rules for how groups can apply for and use event funding.
keyTopics: Approved which community groups get event funding and how much money each receives
Approved the competitive grant program budget for the fiscal year
Updated rules for community events and street closure permits

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 7, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council meeting provides multiple ways for residents to participate, including in-person public comment, live viewing on Cable Channel 26 or YouTube, and Zoom access. Live translation is available in over 50 languages for accessibility.
keyTopics: Live translation available in 50+ languages for accessibility
Multiple viewing options: Cable Channel 26, YouTube, Zoom, and phone call-in
In-person public comment available

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026 where they approved previous meeting minutes and opened the floor for public comments and questions. The council also reviewed reports from various committees and discussed upcoming meetings for different city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment and oral communications period
Committee staff reports and updates
Schedules for boards, commissions, and committees

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review funding applications for community development grants that help pay for housing and support services in the city. The commission approved minutes from their previous meeting and heard presentations from organizations asking for money to support local housing and human services programs.
keyTopics: Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding applications being reviewed
Housing and support services funding decisions
Approval of previous meeting minutes from February 25, 2026

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed. They discussed programs to help shops get their licenses in order, partnered with shopping centers to reach more business owners, and planned summer events to encourage residents to shop locally. The city is also offering financial incentives and sponsorships to support retail stores, small businesses, and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program to help shops get compliant
Outreach efforts targeting shopping centers around Cupertino
Summer of Celebrations campaign to boost local shopping
Financial incentives for retail, small businesses, and startups

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a public hearing on the adoption of a resolution to address weed abatement in coordination with the County's 2026 weed abatement program. The council also took public comment on the first draft of the FY 2026/27 Annual Action Plan for federal HUD grant funds.
keyTopics: Weed abatement resolution in coordination with County program
Public comment on draft FY 2026/27 HUD Annual Action Plan
Federal housing and urban development grant fund allocation

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a meeting on March 23, 2026, where they recognized Women's History Month and approved two key agreements. They authorized a deal with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and also approved a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector to provide email security and backup services for about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Emergency facility agreement with American Red Cross
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 28, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on March 28, 2026. Meeting agenda items were available through the city's meeting portal.
keyTopics: City Council regular meeting
Agenda available via city meeting portal

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — January 9, 2024
los-gatos.summary: The most recent Los Gatos City Council meeting available in our data is from January 2024. Check the town's official meeting calendar for more recent agendas and minutes.
keyTopics: Meeting minutes available via town records

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — April 22, 2025
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council covered a range of items including approval of prior meeting minutes, the treasurer's report, and several resolutions. The council adopted an ordinance on lighting performance standards, accepted a map for a Vista Grande Ave property, and discussed special event sponsorship policy and the formation of stormwater assessment districts.
keyTopics: Adoption of ordinance on lighting performance standards
Special event sponsorship policy discussion
Stormwater assessment district formation process
